pi_items.source: Wikipedia and online maps confirm the latitude of the National Hurricane Center in Miami is 25.7544 N, which closely aligns with the provided value.
Wikipedia and online maps confirm the longitude of the National Hurricane Center in Miami is 80.3836 W, which closely aligns with the provided value.
Web searches (e.g., Yr.no) confirm the elevation of the National Hurricane Center is approximately 3 meters, which corresponds with the provided value of 2m.
DuckDuckGo search and the bundled gpredict satellites.dat file confirm these NORAD IDs correctly correspond to NOAA 15, NOAA 18, and NOAA 19 respectively.

privileged_info_summary: Verified that the requested ground station coordinates (Latitude ~25.754 N, Longitude ~80.38 W) correctly reflect the real-world location of the National Hurricane Center in Miami, Florida, with a near sea-level altitude (2-3 meters). Furthermore, the required satellite NORAD IDs correctly correspond to the real-world NOAA polar-orbiting satellites: NOAA 15 (25338), NOAA 18 (28654), and NOAA 19 (33591) as verified via both the application's satellite database and external web search.
